subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Concert Ticket Line Table L(t) — Rate Estimate, Trapezoidal Average, L'(t)=0 Count & Tickets Sold
stimulus:
Concert tickets went on sale at noon ($t = 0$) and were sold out within 9 hours. The number of people waiting in line to purchase tickets at time $t$ is modeled by a twice-differentiable function $L$ for $0 \le t \le 9$. Values of $L(t)$ at various times $t$ are shown in the table.

| $t$ (hours) | 0 | 1 | 3 | 4 | 7 | 8 | 9 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| $L(t)$ (people) | 120 | 156 | 176 | 126 | 150 | 80 | 0 |

parts.prompt: Use the data in the table to estimate the rate at which the number of people waiting in line was changing at 5:30 P.M. ($t = 5.5$). Show the computations that lead to your answer. Indicate units of measure.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $L'(5.5) \approx \frac{L(7)-L(4)}{7-4} = \frac{150-126}{3} = 8$ people per hour.

parts.prompt: Use a trapezoidal sum with three subintervals to estimate the average number of people waiting in line during the first 4 hours that tickets were on sale.
For $0 \le t \le 9$, what is the fewest number of times at which $L'(t)$ must equal 0? Give a reason for your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $L$ has relative extrema (at minima/maxima on $(0,9)$) — at least one in each of $(1,4)$, $(3,7)$, and $(4,8)$ — so $L'(t) = 0$ for at least 3 values of $t$.

alternatives.correct_example: $L$ increases on $(1,3)$ and $(4,7)$, decreases on $(3,4)$ and $(7,8)$
$L'(t) > 0$ for some $t$ in $(1,3)$ and $(4,7)$, and $L'(t) < 0$ for some $t$ in $(3,4)$ and $(7,8)$; by IVT, $L'(t) = 0$ at least once in each transition
$L'(t) = 0$ for at least $3$ values of $t$ in $[0,9]$

parts.prompt: The rate at which tickets were sold for $0 \le t \le 9$ is modeled by $r(t) = 550te^{-t/2}$ tickets per hour. Based on the model, how many tickets were sold by 3 P.M. ($t = 3$), to the nearest whole number?
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $\int_0^3 550te^{-t/2}\, dt \approx 972.784$; approximately 973 tickets sold by 3 P.M.
